evidence.excerpt: Importing `langchain_text_splitters` eagerly pulls in `nltk`, `spacy`, and `sentence-transformers` (plus `torch` transitively) at module level, adding ~700 MiB RSS even when only lightweight splitters like `RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter` are used.
This regression was introduced in 1.0.0 when [PLC0415](https://docs.astral.sh/ruff/rules/import-outside-top-level/) moved lazy imports to top-level (PR #32325).

Move the three heavy-dep splitter imports (`NLTKTextSplitter`, `SpacyTextSplitter`, `SentenceTransformersTokenTextSplitter`) behind a `__getattr__` lazy loader, following the exact same pattern used throughout `langchain-core` (e.g., `langchain_core.embeddings`, `langchain_core.callbacks`).
Fixes #32252
Putting this complexity into ChatOpenAI to handle a non-OpenAI provider is not warranted.
Merging this PR will **not alter performance**

Convert `raw_lines` to a `collections.deque` at the start of `split_text()` and replace all `.pop(0)` calls with `.popleft()`, which is **O(1)** amortised.
Closing — focusing on bug fixes with higher user impact.
This fixes the streaming bypass of middleware guardrails reported in #35011.
AgentMiddleware gets a new guards_output attribute (default False).
PIIMiddleware sets it when apply_to_output=True.

Hey @ccurme @mdrxy — would appreciate a review when you get a chance. This restores lazy imports for heavy optional dependencies (spacy, nltk, etc.) in `text-splitters` that were accidentally made eager, increasing import time for users who don't use those splitters.
PR #32325 moved these optional dependency imports to module-level `try/except` blocks (to satisfy ruff's `PLC0415` rule).
